profile.profession: Recent commerce graduate looking for first job
profile.personality: uncertain about career direction; somewhat self-doubting; opens up gradually

transcript.content: Hi Anu, I'm Miss Maya. I'm glad you're here to practice English. How was your day?
Hi, Maya. I'm good, thanks. A bit stressed, actually. I have an exam tomorrow. How are you?
Hi Anu, I understand exams can be stressful. What subject is it? Take your time—no rush at all.
It's for a job placement test. I'm worried I won't do well. My confidence is low. Have you ever felt like this? 😕
That sounds tough, but you're doing great just by preparing. I have felt like this before—before my own exams. Small fix: we say "I have felt like this." What are you studying right now?
I'm studying commerce—accounting, economics, and some basic management. It's a lot to remember. Ma'am, sometimes I feel like I'm not good enough. 😔
You’re doing important work, Anu. Many people feel that way before exams. Small fix: we say “I am not good enough.” What part of commerce interests you most?
Honestly? I like accounting the most. It feels logical, you know? But sometimes I get confused between different formulas. I think I need more practice. 😅
I get that—accounting can be tricky with all the numbers. Small fix: we say “I think I need more practice.” What’s one formula that gives you the most trouble?

The compound interest formula, for sure. I always mix up the time and rate part. Like, is it multiplied or divided? It’s so frustrating. 😩 (And sometimes I think, *yaar, why can’t it be simple like Telugu math?*)
Compound interest can be confusing, but you’ll get better with practice. Small fix: we say 'why can’t it be as simple as Telugu math?' What helps you remember formulas best?

Hmm, I guess writing them down repeatedly helps. Or maybe making little notes with examples. Oh, and sometimes watching YouTube videos. But I get distracted easily. 😅 (Ya, I know, I’m my own problem.)
Writing and examples are great methods. Small fix: we say “I am my own problem.” What YouTube channel do you watch for help?

I follow some Indian channels, like “Merit Batch” or “Commerce Wallah.” They explain things clearly, but I often end up watching reels instead. It’s a bad habit, I know. 😅 (Yaar, why is self-discipline so hard?)
It’s true—self-discipline is hard! Small fix: we say “Why is self-discipline so hard?” How do you plan to stay focused tomorrow?
I dunno, Maya. Maybe I’ll set strict time limits and put my phone in another room. But what if I get bored? I mean, exams are already stressful. 😅 (Ya, I’m panicking a little.)
Hi Anu, I understand exams can be stressful. What subject is it? Take your time—no rush at all.
Maybe I'll try making a study playlist to keep me motivated. But honestly, I'm just trying to get through this. Thanks for listening, Maya. 😅 (Ya, I’m still panicking a bit.)
A study playlist sounds like a good idea. Small fix: we say “I am still panicking a bit.” What songs do you usually add to your playlists?

enjoys the cool post-lunch break feeling
cooks as a form of relaxation
has not been to Chennai but would love to experience that vibe

facts.learning_methods: writing things down repeatedly, making notes with examples, watching YouTube videos (prefers Merit Batch and Commerce Wallah channels)
facts.known_challenges: compound interest formula (mixes up time and rate, unclear if multiplied or divided)
facts.distraction_pattern: gets distracted easily, often watches reels instead of studying
facts.self_perception: struggles with self-discipline, self-critical about focus issues
